meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional project.
content.answer_block: Transform your blank portfolio into a data-driven showcase by integrating professional analytics. By tracking user events in your first app, you demonstrate that you understand not just how to code, but how to measure success, growth, and user behavior—the exact skills hiring managers demand from junior developers.
content.intro:
You stare at the empty 'Projects' section of your GitHub, feeling the weight of a thousand tutorials that never quite translated into a real-world asset. The industry doesn't care about your 'To-Do List' app; it cares about data, engagement, and your ability to prove your code makes a tangible impact.

Today, we flip the script. We are moving beyond simple CRUD apps by injecting professional-grade analytics into your project. This isn't just about tracking clicks; it's about building a narrative for your portfolio that says, 'I build software that matters.'

steps.title: Define Your Metrics Strategy
steps.description: Identify which user actions actually prove value. Focus on conversion events rather than vanity metrics like page views.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Suggest 3 high-value user behavior events to track for a personal SaaS dashboard project to demonstrate analytical depth to potential employers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a metrics_schema.json file defining event names, properties, and the business logic behind each tracking point.'

steps.title: Integrate Tracking Middleware
steps.description: Abstract your tracking logic into a reusable service so your main application code remains clean and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware for an Express app that captures user interaction events and pipes them to a mock analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement an AnalyticsService class that handles asynchronous event logging without blocking the main UI thread.'

steps.title: Create a Data Visualization Dashboard
steps.description: Build a simple UI to display these events. This turns raw logs into an impressive 'Admin View' for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React component using Recharts to visualize the last 24 hours of user events captured by our AnalyticsService.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a dashboard page component that fetches event logs and renders a bar chart for daily active users.'

steps.title: Automate Event Testing
steps.description: Ensure your analytics are firing correctly by writing automated tests for your tracking events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Jest test suite that verifies the AnalyticsService triggers the correct API call when a user clicks the 'Purchase' button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run tests for event tracking and verify that all mocks return a 200 OK status code.'

steps.title: Deploy and Document Results
steps.description: Host your app and write a README that explains the 'Why' behind your analytics choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional 'Technical Case Study' README section explaining why I chose to implement these specific analytics events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-impact README.md that highlights the analytics architecture and business insights derived from the data.'

pro_tips: Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information). Always hash user IDs before sending data to third-party analytics providers.
Implement 'Sampling' in your analytics service to keep costs low while maintaining statistically significant data.
Use custom event properties to capture context, like the user's browser or the specific version of your app they are using.
Create a 'Dark Mode' toggle for your analytics dashboard to show off your CSS proficiency alongside your data skills.
Add a 'Performance Monitor' event to track how long your API calls take; this shows you care about latency and user experience.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, share the link on LinkedIn, and pin the 'Case Study' README to your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You now have a project that speaks the language of business. Stop building tutorials and start building products that show you are ready for a professional engineering role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
